title: Wonderbolt Starfighter
content:
The Cloudsdale Airtech Wonderbolt Space Superiority Fighter was an Equestrian single-seat starfighter used by the the armed forces of [[United Equus]] before and during the The Great War, and by local planetary defense forces for a number of years after. Designed in 2424, five years prior to the outbreak of the war, it was a landmark achievement for Equus, as it was the planet's first viable starfighter to be produced in large numbers.

Despite it's importance in the technological history of Equus, it was largely a failure in it's intended role as a space superiority fighter. During the Great War, squadrons of Wonderbolts consistently suffered high losses in space engagements, and underpreformed compared to it's contemporaries. Regardless of it's abject failure as a space superiority fighter, it was found to excel in atmospheric settings, leading to it's widespread use by the Equus Planetary Forces, despite originally being created for the navy.

Much of the failures (and unexpected success) of the Wonderbolt can be attributed to it's designer, Cloudsdale Airtech, an aeronautics firm with little prior experience with spacecraft. Founded in the late 9th century CYP (early 23rd century CE) as a guild specializing in pegasi chariots and carts, Cloudsdale Airtech historically was an early adopter of advances in aeronautics, and were among the first to utilize technologies created for space travel in other industries.

However, due to their (and by-and-large, the rest of Equus as well) lack of experience with the realities of starfighter combat, the Wonderbolt was designed with Equestrian air combat in mind, as was their only form of reference at the time. This disconnect between the design philosophy and the intended use of the Wonderbolt is what lead to the fighter's poor space performance during the Great War.

Ironically, the other belligerents in the Great War suffered a similar issue with their own vessels, just in the opposite direction. Equus' enemies and allies, long acclimated to space combat being the defining action in a war, paid little attention to atmospheric craft. After all, in preceding conflicts, starfighters were enough to deal with the limited planetary engagements seen prior. However, large-scale planetary action quickly became commonplace during the great, and the Wonderbolt found its niche. Designed with the principals of a pre-FTL airplane, it easily dominated enemy starfighters in atmospheric settings.
